what_is_open: Derive a valid closed-form welfare-regret upper bound for the genuinely feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm under the frozen assumptions, or else prove that no such bound of the advertised balancing form can hold on the present class.
obstruction: The route encoded in the target statement uses the exact drift identity from lem:clip-bias, but then makes a localization step that is not justified by the frozen primitives. The drift factor is (bar e_q-e_P)(Delta_1/bar e_q + Delta_0/(1-bar e_q)). To turn this into a balanceable clipped-region term one must control the clipping displacement bar e_q-bar e by the true rare-overlap set {p_P<=q}. However bar e_q-bar e is supported on {bar e<q} union {bar e>1-q}, an event defined by the estimated propensity, while ass:marginal-overlap controls only the true-law event {p_P<=q}. Assumption ass:nuisance-rate gives only global L2 bounds on ehat-e_P and muhat-mu, not the q-localized sup-norm or margin-type control needed to compare these events. Consequently the claimed bound B(q) <= r_mu_n r_e_n / q + r_mu_n q^{1/(2 gamma)} is not derivable from the frozen dependencies. The gamma=0 endpoint is also not closed by ass:marginal-overlap, because its stated convention gives no vanishing q-tail to optimize against.
attempted: Route 1: apply Cauchy-Schwarz directly to the exact drift identity. This yields |E[(pi-pi_star)b_q]| <= q^{-1} ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 (||Delta_0||_2+||Delta_1||_2), but leaves ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 nonlocal. Route 2: split ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 <= ||bar e-e_P||_2 + ||bar e_q-bar e||_2 and try to bound the second term by q P{p_P<=q}^{1/2}. This fails because the support of bar e_q-bar e is determined by bar e, not by p_P. Route 3: combine thm:margin-localization with ass:vc-localized-envelope to control the oracle empirical-process term. This recovers the usual localized stochastic scale n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} rho^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} polylog(n), but it does not resolve the feasible drift. Route 4: use only the global L2 bound ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 <= ||bar e-e_P||_2 + 1, which is too crude to yield any meaningful optimization in q or any frontier-advancing rate theorem.
partial_result: Two pieces are proved. First, thm:minimax-lower gives the sharp converse benchmark M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}. Second, the achievability route is reduced exactly to the product-form clipped-score drift from lem:clip-bias together with the oracle localized empirical-process term supplied by thm:margin-localization and ass:vc-localized-envelope. Concretely, for a regret radius rho, the stochastic offset term has oracle scale A_n(q,rho) of order n^{-1/2} q^{-1/2} rho^{alpha/(2+2 alpha)} polylog(n), while the feasible obstruction is entirely in controlling ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 inside q^{-1} ||bar e_q-e_P||_2 (||Delta_0||_2+||Delta_1||_2). Any next-round resolution must therefore add a new argument that localizes the estimated clipping event to the true weak-overlap region, or else show by counterexample that such a localization is impossible on the current core.

what_is_open: Determine whether any genuinely feasible estimator, and in particular the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, can attain the converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) under weak overlap, or whether feasible weak-arm nuisance learning necessarily forces a strictly slower welfare-regret rate.
obstruction: This tightness question cannot be settled before oeq:feasible-upper is closed. The present frozen core proves the lower benchmark r_star through thm:minimax-lower, but it proves no feasible upper rate and no feasible lower obstruction beyond the minimax converse. Without either an achievability theorem at exponent r_star or a separate impossibility theorem showing every feasible method is slower, the comparison required for tightness is mathematically undetermined.
attempted: I reduced the question to the precise obstruction in oeq:feasible-upper. Every attempted resolution of tightness first requires a feasible upper theorem, because the only proved rate statement presently available is the converse. No additional argument from the current dependencies yields a feasible-achievability exponent or a feasible-only lower bound.
partial_result: The converse exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) is fully identified and proved sharp as a minimax lower benchmark on the full class by thm:minimax-lower. The exact source of the feasible difficulty is also identified: it is the nonlocal product drift in lem:clip-bias, not the oracle empirical-process term. Thus the residual open problem is sharply formulated: either devise a feasible estimator and proof that overcome that drift at rate n^{-r_star}, or prove that this is impossible and a slower feasible frontier prevails.
